description: Make the DS-160 CEAC worker actually executable and testable in production by wiring the live worker to the new CEAC flow, surfacing anti-bot/manual gates honestly, and adding runtime-verifiable smoke diagnostics.

userStories.title: Pin CEAC runtime-validation scope and truth contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the follow-up CEAC runtime-validation scope codified so Ralph works on the real worker/runtime gap instead of assuming the prior PRD proved live autofill.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/prd-ds160-ceac-runtime-validation.md exists in the repo
prd.json contains CEAC runtime-validation stories US-009 through US-014
The repo branch for this run is ralph/ds160-ceac-runtime-validation
The PRD explicitly states that CEAC anti-bot / captcha gates are in scope as operational blockers and must be surfaced honestly
userStories.notes: Docs-only anchor story for the new runtime-validation pass. Do not claim live autofill proof until the worker path and smoke diagnostics are real.

userStories.title: Replace the legacy DS-160 worker path with the CEAC runtime pipeline
userStories.description: As ops, I need processDs160Item to use the new CEAC session/navigation/result modules instead of the legacy prefillDs160 prototype so the live worker follows the intended stop-at-sign architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: viza-be/submission-service/src/index.ts no longer relies on the legacy prefillDs160() prototype as the primary DS-160 execution path
The DS-160 queue processor starts from the CEAC session/bootstrap flow and returns the typed CEAC run result contract
The success path is structurally aligned with stop-at-sign / handoff_ready rather than final submission
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core wiring story. Keep scope inside submission-service; do not redesign unrelated queue systems.

userStories.title: Detect CEAC anti-bot and manual-intervention gates explicitly
userStories.description: As ops, I need the worker to detect CEAC anti-bot pages, captcha/manual gates, and unsupported start-page states explicitly so the runtime reports blocked/manual-required honestly instead of pretending autofill ran.
acceptanceCriteria: The CEAC start flow detects anti-bot or manual-gate pages explicitly rather than classifying them as generic unknown-page failures
Worker output/status distinguishes blocked/manual-required from retriable worker failure
Captured diagnostics include useful operator context such as visible page text, support ID, or other gate markers when available
The worker avoids silent retry loops when the blocker is an external CEAC anti-bot/manual gate
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not attempt to auto-solve captchas. The goal is truthful detection and clean handoff/manual escalation.

userStories.title: Implement real page-by-page CEAC fill orchestration on the new flow
userStories.description: As VIZA, I need the new CEAC runtime path to actually drive page-by-page filling, navigation, save/checkpoint behavior, and stop-at-sign behavior so the worker can perform the intended DS-160 autofill when CEAC allows it.
acceptanceCriteria: The wired CEAC runtime path fills supported answer groups through the new navigation/checkpoint helpers instead of the prototype loop
Application ID capture, save checkpoints, .dat capture, and sign-page artifact capture are reachable from the live worker path
Failure paths preserve recovery metadata and diagnostics through the new result/failure contract
No code path enters passport signature, solves the final captcha, or clicks final Sign and Submit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is about the live worker path only. Keep the stop-at-sign contract intact.

userStories.title: Persist CEAC runtime outcomes and queue semantics clearly
userStories.description: As ops, I need the worker to persist handoff-ready, blocked/manual-required, and failed CEAC outcomes clearly so queue state and application metadata reflect what really happened.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful handoff-ready runs persist the CEAC result payload and any available Application ID / retrieval / artifact metadata to the existing application/queue records
Blocked/manual-required outcomes are stored distinctly from generic failure and include operator-facing context
Retry and alert behavior differentiates external CEAC gating from genuine worker/runtime failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Prefer minimally invasive persistence changes. Keep existing Supabase tables unless a small targeted extension is truly required.

userStories.title: Add operator-verifiable CEAC smoke diagnostics and test instructions
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a repeatable smoke/diagnostic path that proves whether CEAC start-page access is available, anti-bot gated, or otherwise blocked so we can test runtime readiness honestly from the machine.
acceptanceCriteria: submission-service includes a documented smoke/diagnostic entry point for CEAC start-page probing
The smoke path emits a machine-readable outcome such as start_page, anti_bot_gate, or blocked with supporting details
Docs explain how to run the smoke test and how to interpret blocked/manual-required outcomes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is about truthful runtime verification, not mocking a fake success. Prefer small scripts/docs over heavyweight test frameworks.
